title: kernel.inspector.* Tauri IPC bridge for Diagnostics-panel access
goal: Bridge the inspector_read trait to Tauri IPC so the Diagnostics panel (cluster X) reads inspector state without binding to the local HTTP server (the panel runs in-process and skips the network hop). IPC channels: `kernel.inspector.port`, `kernel.inspector.list_sessions`, `kernel.inspector.session_state`, `kernel.inspector.event_ledger_tail`, `kernel.inspector.process_ledger_active`, `kernel.inspector.trace_projection`, `kernel.inspector.loaded_models`.
expected_diff_shape: New Tauri command file app/src-tauri/src/inspector.rs with seven commands; each delegates to InspectorReadV1 trait impl; tests confirm IPC payload shapes round-trip cleanly.
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.5.2 the Diagnostics panel (cluster X) needs read access without going through the HTTP server (network hop unnecessary in-process). The Tauri commands wrap the InspectorReadV1 trait methods one-to-one. Each command serializes the returned struct to JSON via serde. The handlers take a `State<Arc<dyn InspectorReadV1>>` parameter populated at Tauri::Builder setup. Channel ids per spec §6.5 + MT-010 manual convention: `kernel.inspector.port` (returns the random port from MT-029 server if running, else None), `kernel.inspector.list_sessions`, `kernel.inspector.session_state(session_id: SessionId)`, `kernel.inspector.event_ledger_tail(n: usize)`, `kernel.inspector.process_ledger_active`, `kernel.inspector.trace_projection(session_id: SessionId)`, `kernel.inspector.loaded_models`. Each command must appear in the ModelManual (per HBR-MAN-001 paired-diff — the commit adding these commands must update content.rs). Tests: instantiate a Tauri test harness, invoke each command via IPC mock, assert response payload deserializes back to the same struct type returned by the trait method.
risk_if_missed: Without IPC bridge, Diagnostics panel must hit HTTP for in-process reads — adds latency and an unnecessary network surface.
validator_focus: All seven IPC channels are added to ModelManual content in the same commit (HBR-MAN-001 paired-diff gate will block otherwise); IPC payload shapes round-trip cleanly; State<Arc<dyn InspectorReadV1>> is the single source of read truth.

minimum_controls: Seven commands added to ModelManual in same commit (HBR-MAN-001 gate).
State<Arc<dyn InspectorReadV1>> is the single read source (no duplicate impls).
IPC round-trip tested for every command.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: All seven Tauri inspector IPC commands are wired one-to-one against InspectorReadV1 and the ModelManual lists them as Wired, but the contract requires a `kernel.inspector.workspace_state_read` channel and the implementation omits the workspace_state_read command from the IPC bridge — six of seven trait methods are exposed.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: All seven Tauri inspector IPC commands are wired one-to-one against InspectorReadV1 and the ModelManual lists them as Wired, but the contract requires a `kernel.inspector.workspace_state_read` channel and the implementation omits the workspace_state_read command from the IPC bridge — six of seven trait methods are exposed.
